---
title: "QC metrics for interpretation"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{QC metrics for interpretation}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

Spectreasy persists measured values and their units without assigning
scientific quality labels. Existing report tables include control gating and
stain-index summaries, spectral similarity, negative-population spread,
detector residuals, sample reconstruction error, reference spectra, and AF
spectra.

Additional compact tables provide robust positive/negative separation and
spectral consistency per control, control-spectrum quantiles, AF-bank rank and
pairwise similarity, and sample AF-band usage when those measurements are
available. Large matrices remain CSV files and are represented in the
paste-ready prompt by derived summaries. WLS/RWLS weighted residuals are never
described as directly comparable with raw residuals, and spectral similarity is
treated as panel-complexity evidence rather than an automatic defect.
